segments.p1-10-g22: A simple interjection affirming what was just said; here it functions as an emphatic 'yes'.
segments.p1-10-g23: Present-tense first-person of отвечать ('I answer/I reply'), placed as a parenthetical remark between dashes; я is the nominative subject, added for emphasis or clarity.
segments.p1-10-g24: Conditional clause introduced by если + the contracted conditional particle б (если б = 'if ... would'); надо было is an impersonal construction meaning 'it would be necessary' (literally 'it was necessary') and governs the infinitive жить; только для того = 'only for the purpose (of that)'.
segments.p1-10-g25: чтоб (colloquial form of чтобы) introduces the purpose/result clause 'in order (that)'; the negative particle не negates the perfective infinitive замочиться 'to get soaked/get wet'. The infinitive expresses the undesired outcome to be avoided.
segments.p1-10-g26: A rhetorical question: Но 'but' + что делать with the emphatic particle же gives the idiomatic meaning 'But what is to be done?' or 'What can you do?'; делать is the infinitive 'to do' used impersonally.
segments.p1-10-g27: A conditional subordinate clause: если 'if' + past tense забрал (perfective) 'I took/seized' with себе (dative) yields the idiom 'забрал себе в голову' — 'got it into my head' or 'took it into my head' (i.e., convinced myself of something); в голову here can mean 'into my mind.'
segments.p1-10-g28: This clause reports the content of that thought: что introduces a nominal clause 'that (people) live...', живут (3rd-person plural) 'they/people live', and the sequence и не для одного этого means 'not for this one thing alone' (и here reinforces/links the clauses). The second что begins another reported idea, followed by the conditional phrase если уж жить where уж is an intensifier ('if one is to live at all/indeed').
segments.p1-10-g29: tak уж жить is a conclusion 'then/if so, indeed live' (так уж = 'in that case' / 'if one must'), followed by the locative plural в хоромах meaning 'in mansions' or 'in fine rooms' (хоромах is somewhat archaic/literary). The infinitive жить is used generally ('to live' / 'one should live').
segments.p1-10-g30: Это is the demonstrative pronoun 'this' used as a predicative introducer ('this is ...'). Мое is the possessive pronoun 'my' placed before the noun. Хотение is an older/formal noun meaning 'will/desire' and serves as the nominal predicate; the clause reads literally 'This (is) my will.'
segments.p1-10-g31: This repeats the thought for emphasis: это 'this' again as a demonstrative predicative, желание 'desire' is a synonymous noun, and мое is the possessive repeated. Note the more marked word order in 'желание мое' (possessive after the noun) which gives a slightly emphatic or literary tone.
segments.p1-10-g32: Вы is the 2nd-person plural (or formal singular) subject 'you'. Его here is an accusative pronoun meaning 'it/him' (object) referring back to the previously mentioned feeling. Выскоблите is the perfective future, 2nd-person-plural form 'you will scrape (off/out)'; из + меня is the preposition 'out of/from' with меня in the genitive ('out of me'). Только тогда is a temporal limiter 'only then/only in that case.'
segments.p1-10-g33: Когда is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing the condition. Перемените is perfective future, 2nd-person-plural 'you will change' (so the change is viewed as a single completed act). Желания мои is the direct object 'my desires'; note again the possessive following the noun (мои → 'my') for slightly emphatic/literary effect.
segments.p1-10-g65: Уничтожьте is the 2nd-person plural (or polite singular) imperative of уничтожить — a perfective verb meaning 'destroy/annihilate.' Мои желания is the direct object in the accusative plural (мои = possessive 'my'; желания = 'desires'). The whole segment is a direct command addressed to 'you'.
segments.p1-10-g66: Сотрите is the 2nd-person plural imperative of стереть, here used figuratively as 'wipe out/erase.' Мои идеалы is again the accusative plural object ('my ideals'). The verb is perfective/completeive in sense: erase them completely.
segments.p1-10-g67: Покажите is the 2nd-person plural imperative of показать 'to show.' Мне is the dative 'to me.' Что-нибудь is the indefinite pronoun formed from что + нибудь, meaning 'something/anything.' Лучше is the comparative ('better') used here to modify that indefinite object: 'something better.'
segments.p1-10-g68: И = 'and.' Я is the first-person subject. За вами with the instrumental pronoun вами means 'behind/after you' (i.e., following you). Пойду is the first-person singular future of пойти (perfective 'to go/set off') and here means 'I will follow/go after you.' Together: 'and I will follow you.'
segments.p1-10-g69: Вы is the polite/plural 2nd-person pronoun 'you' used as the subject or addressee. It marks the person the speaker is addressing.
segments.p1-10-g70: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps/probably' or softening the assertion here ('you will probably say...'). It modifies the following verb.
segments.p1-10-g71: Скажете is the 2nd-person plural future of сказать: 'you will say.' Used here to predict or attribute reported speech to the addressee.
segments.p1-10-g72: Что is the conjunction 'that' introducing reported content. Не стоит is an impersonal construction from стоить meaning 'it is (not) worth'; with the infinitive связываться it means 'it's not worth getting involved.' И here functions as an emphatic particle 'even' (i.e., 'not even worth'). Связываться is the reflexive infinitive 'to get involved/enter into dealings.'
segments.p1-10-g73: Но = 'but.' В таком случае = 'in such a case' (в + demonstrative таком + case). Ведь is a particle 'after all/you know' adding persuasion. И here means 'also/even.' Я вам могу ответить is a modal construction 'I can/could answer you'; тем же ответить = 'answer with the same [thing/in the same way],' where тем (demonstrative/pronoun) + же (emphatic particle) refer back to what was just mentioned. The infinitive ответить completes the construction.
segments.p1-10-g74: Мы is the 1st-person-plural subject 'we'. Рассуждаем is present-tense, 1st-plural 'we reason/discuss' (a habitual or ongoing present). Серьезно is an adverb 'seriously' modifying the verb.
segments.p1-10-g75: А is a contrastive conjunction 'and/but'. Не хотите is present 2nd-person-plural negative 'you do not want' (polite or plural). Меня is the direct object 'me'. Удостоить is a perfective infinitive 'to bestow/honor (with)', and here takes an instrumental phrase to mark what is bestowed: вашим вниманием ('with your attention'), where вашим is the possessive 'your' and вниманием is the instrument (attention) in the instrumental case.
segments.p1-10-g76: Так here is best read as 'then/in that case' setting the result; ведь is a discourse particle 'after all/you know' adding emphasis. Кланяться is the infinitive 'to bow', and не буду is the 1st-person-singular future construction 'I will not' (буду + infinitive = future), so the clause means 'I will not bow.'
segments.p1-10-g77: This is a short possessive construction: У + genitive pronoun + есть + noun. У меня is the standard Russian way to express possession ('I have'), where у is a preposition used idiomatically (not spatial) and меня is the genitive of я. Есть here is the present-tense existential/identificational verb ('is'/'there is') used in the formula 'у меня есть' = 'I have.' Подполье is the possessed noun (here a physical subterranean room or cellar).
segments.p1-10-g1: Вы is the 2nd‑person pronoun (formal/plural) addressing the reader; верите is present tense, 2nd‑person indicative of верить ‘believe’; в takes the accusative, so хрустальное здание (neuter adjective + neuter noun in accusative = nominative form) is the direct object. The clause is a simple declarative (rhetorical) statement.
segments.p1-10-g2: навеки is an adverb meaning 'forever' and modifies the following adjective; нерушимое is an indeclinable‑looking full adjective in the neuter form agreeing with здание and means 'indestructible' — an epithet after the noun, separated by a comma for emphasis.
segments.p1-10-g3: то есть is the fixed idiom 'that is' (то + есть where есть functions as the copula in the idiom); в такое uses в + accusative/ demonstrative такое ('such a one') referring back to 'хрустальное здание' — it restates/clarifies what the speaker means by 'crystal building'.
segments.p1-10-g4: которому is the dative singular relative pronoun ('to which' / 'to whom') linking back to 'такое/здание'; нельзя будет is an impersonal future construction: нельзя (impersonal 'it is impossible' / 'one cannot') + future auxiliary будет; ни ... выставить begins a negative pair with ни ... ни ... and emphasizes 'not even'; языка is used for 'tongue' (here part of the idiom 'языка выставить' — 'to stick out one's tongue'); украдкой is an adverb 'stealthily'; выставить is the infinitive 'to stick out / display'.
segments.p1-10-g5: The second member of the correlated negative pair: ни marks 'not even'; кукиша (diminutive кукиш) is the figurative 'fig sign' (a rude gesture) functioning as the object; в кармане is a locative phrase ('in the pocket') — кармане is the prepositional-like locative form used after в in colloquial speech; показать is the infinitive 'to show'. Together with the previous clause it lists acts that will be impossible toward that building.
segments.p1-10-g6: Ну is an interjection meaning 'well' or 'now', used to introduce or soften the speaker's remark.
segments.p1-10-g7: а is a contrastive conjunction meaning 'and / but / as for'; я is the 1st‑person pronoun 'I' — together they set up the contrast 'and I (on the other hand)'.
segments.p1-10-g8: может быть is the common modal phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'maybe' (literally 'it may be') and qualifies the following statement.
segments.p1-10-g9: потому-то is an emphatic form of потому ('for that reason') where the particle то adds emphasis ('for that very reason'); и here adds a linking/emphatic force; боюсь is present tense 'I fear / I am afraid of'; этого здания is genitive 'of this building' (object of боюсь).
segments.p1-10-g10: This is a double subordinate clause introduced by что ('that'): first it states the property of the building (оно 'it' + хрустальное и навеки нерушимое — 'crystal and forever indestructible'), then a second что introduces the impersonal impossibility clause (нельзя будет 'it will be impossible') with даже и + украдкой adding emphasis. ему is the dative 'to it' (i.e. toward the building) and выставить is the infinitive 'to stick out / display' — together: 'that one will not even be able to furtively stick out a tongue at it.'
segments.p1-10-g11: Вот видите ли is a conversational opening: вот (here/now/well) + видите (2nd‑person present of видеть) used idiomatically as 'you see / you understand', and the enclitic ли softens it to a rhetorical 'you see (don't you)'.
segments.p1-10-g12: если introduces a conditional 'if'; вместо takes the genitive (дворца) and means 'instead of'; будет + курятник is a common impersonal/existential future 'there will be a henhouse' with курятник as the nominative subject; и пойдет дождь uses пойдет (pf. of пойти) meaning 'it will start / begin' to rain — an idiomatic impersonal phrasing.
segments.p1-10-g13: я is the 1st‑person pronoun 'I' and marks the subject of the following verbs.
segments.p1-10-g14: может быть (as above) is the modal 'perhaps / maybe', indicating possibility or tentativeness about the action that follows.
segments.p1-10-g15: и here adds an intensifying/ concessive nuance ('might even'); влезу is the 1st‑person singular future of влезть 'I will climb in / get into'; в + курятник is the motion into the henhouse (accusative).
segments.p1-10-g16: чтоб (colloquial for чтобы) introduces purpose: 'so that / in order that'; не + замочиться is the perfective reflexive infinitive 'not to get soaked' — the purpose of entering the henhouse.
segments.p1-10-g17: но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; все-таки is a set phrase meaning 'nevertheless / all the same' (here split into 'все' + 'таки'); курятника appears in the genitive (городской/partitive/colloquial coloring) where one might expect an accusative — it's a colloquial/idiomatic choice but still names the henhouse as the thing not to be accepted; не приму is future negated 'I will not accept'; за дворец is 'as a palace' (за + accusative marks 'to regard as'); из благодарности is the adverbial 'out of gratitude'.
segments.p1-10-g18: что introduces the explanatory clause 'that it saved me from the rain'; он refers back to the henhouse (grammatically 'he/it'), меня is the accusative 'me', от дождя is the 'from the rain' phrase (от + genitive), and сохранил is the perfective past 'saved / preserved'.
segments.p1-10-g19: Вы смеетесь is a direct address: Вы (2nd‑person pronoun) + смеетесь (present 2nd‑person of смеяться) meaning 'you are laughing' (or 'you laugh').
segments.p1-10-g20: вы even повторено for emphasis; даже is the adverb 'even' adding emphasis; говорите is present 2nd‑person 'you say' — it introduces the reported content in the next segment.
segments.p1-10-g21: что introduces the reported content 'that'; в этом случае is the locative phrase 'in this case' (в + demonstrative + prepositional/locative); курятник и хоромы lists the two items being compared ('a henhouse and mansions'); все равно is the idiom 'all the same / it makes no difference' (все 'everything' + равно 'equal').
segments.p1-10-g34: The particle Ну here is a colloquial prompt/introductory word urging the addressee on (roughly 'come on' / 'well then') and sets an impatient or cajoling tone for the imperatives that follow.
segments.p1-10-g35: Перемените is the 2nd‑person plural imperative (or polite plural) of переменить 'to change' — a direct command to change something; the direct object is omitted here and is supplied by the prior context (my desires).
segments.p1-10-g36: Прельстите is the 2nd‑person plural imperative of прельстить 'to entice/tempt' (here not primarily 'to deceive'); меня is the accusative object 'me'; другим is instrumental and functions like 'with another (thing)' or 'by something else' (an implicit noun is understood).
segments.p1-10-g37: Дайте is the 2nd‑person plural imperative of дать 'to give/provide'; мне is the dative 'to me'; другой идеал is the accusative noun phrase 'another / a different ideal' (другой modifies идеал).
segments.p1-10-g38: This whole clause: А is a contrastive conjunction 'but'; покамест is an archaic/dated adverb meaning 'for the time being' or 'while'; я is the 1st‑person subject; уж is an emphatic particle ('indeed' / 'at any rate') strengthening the negation; не приму is the 1st‑person singular verb (future of принять) with negation — 'I will not accept/take'; курятника is the noun in genitive used with the construction принимать ... за (here effectively 'to take X for Y'); за дворец completes the idiom принимать ... за meaning 'to take (something) for/as a (different) thing' — 'to mistake a chicken coop for a palace.'
segments.p1-10-g39: Пусть here is the concessive particle 'let' / 'suppose' used to concede a point; даже intensifies ('even'); так будет is a short clause 'let it be so' (будет is 3rd‑person singular future/present used with пусть to express 'let it be').
segments.p1-10-g40: This introduces a subordinate clause: что 'that'; хрустальное здание 'the crystal building' (adjective + noun); есть is the emphatic copula (literary/present 'is') used here to link subject and predicate; пуф is the nominal predicate — literally 'a pouf/ottoman' (used figuratively here).
segments.p1-10-g41: This continues with another 'that' clause: что 'that'; по законам природы is a prepositional phrase 'according to the laws of nature' (по + dative); его refers back to the crystal building ('it'); и не полагается is an idiomatic impersonal/passive construction meaning 'is not warranted/does not properly obtain' (полагается here = 'is to be expected / is justified'); и что я выдумал его 'and that I made it up' — я (subject) выдумал (past tense, 'I invented/made up') его 'it'; только вследствие моей собственной глупости 'only as a consequence of my own stupidity' (вследствие governs genitive).
segments.p1-10-g42: Вследствие 'due to / as a consequence of' governs the genitive; некоторых is the genitive plural of 'some / certain'; старинных is the genitive plural adjective 'old' or 'old‑fashioned' modifying the following noun (continued in the next segment).
segments.p1-10-g43: Нерациональных is genitive plural 'irrational' (modifying привычек); привычек is genitive plural 'habits' (governed by вследствие); нашего поколенiя is the possessive phrase 'of our generation' (нашего genitive sing. of наш, поколенiя genitive 'generation').
segments.p1-10-g44: Но is a contrastive conjunction 'but'; какое ... дело is an idiomatic interrogative/indefinite phrase 'what business / what concern is it'; мне is dative 'to me' and pairs with дело to mean 'as for me / what concern is it to me'.
segments.p1-10-g45: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that...'; его refers back to the crystal building 'it'; не полагается repeats the idiomatic expression 'is not warranted / is not appropriate / ought not to be' (полагается here is impersonal/passive in sense 'is proper/justified').
segments.p1-10-g104: Неужели is an interrogative/exclamatory particle expressing surprise or disbelief ('could it be / really?'). в этом is a prepositional phrase with в + demonstrative pronoun это in the prepositional case ('in/with regard to this' = 'that'), and вся цель is a feminine nominal phrase where вся (feminine form of весь) modifies цель ('the whole/entire aim'); the phrase as a whole asks incredulously whether that is the whole aim.
segments.p1-10-g105: Не is the simple negation placed before the verb; верю is the 1st person singular present of верить ('I believe'). The subject 'I' is omitted (understood from the verb form), so the short sentence means 'I don't believe (it/that).'
segments.p1-10-g106: А here is a coordinating conjunction used colloquially to introduce or contrast what follows ('well' / 'but'); it doesn't form a full clause by itself and is a discourse marker.
segments.p1-10-g107: Впрочем is an adverbial particle that softens or adjusts the speaker's remark ('however', 'actually' / 'anyway'); here it signals a slight change or emphasis before the following direct address.
segments.p1-10-g108: Знаете is 2nd person plural (or polite) present of знать used as a conversational opener ('you know'); что here functions as an exclamatory/introductory particle in the fixed phrase 'знаете что' = 'you know what:' introducing the upcoming statement.
segments.p1-10-g109: Я is the explicit first-person pronoun 'I' (nominative). Убежден is a short-form past participial/adjectival predicate meaning 'convinced' or 'I'm convinced' (masculine); together they form the main clause 'I am convinced,' introducing the subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-10-g110: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. На нашего брата подпольного is a noun phrase meaning 'our underground brother'; because the noun is animate masculine the accusative form matches the genitive form (нашего брата), and подпольного is the agreeing adjective. Нужно is an impersonal predicate meaning 'it is necessary' or 'must' that governs the infinitive держать ('to keep/hold'); в узде is an idiomatic prepositional phrase literally 'in the bridle' meaning 'under control' or 'on a tight rein'. The infinitive держать completes the construction 'нужно держать в узде' = 'must keep (in check)'.
segments.p1-10-g111: Он is the subject pronoun 'he'. Хоть и is a concessive conjunctional pair meaning 'even if' / 'though', with и reinforcing the concession. Способен is the short-form adjective 'capable/able' and is followed by the perfective infinitive просидеть ('to sit out / to sit for') which here expresses the ability to endure a long duration; молча ('silently') and в подполье ('in the underground') give manner and location, and сорок лет specifies duration. Word order is flexible; the infinitive + numeric duration emphasizes endurance.
segments.p1-10-g112: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'. Уж is an emphatic particle ('indeed' / 'once'); коль is an archaic/colloquial conditional conjunction meaning 'if' or 'once'. Выйдет is the perfective future 3rd-person of выйти ('comes out/comes into view'); на свет is a frozen phrase 'into the light' / 'into the world'; да is an archaic coordinating particle 'and' linking to the next verb; прорвется is perfective future reflexive 'will break through' or 'burst out'. Together they form the conditional clause 'but if he comes out into the light and bursts forth...'.
segments.p1-10-g113: Так is an adverb here meaning 'so' / 'then' or 'in that case'. Уж again adds emphasis ('indeed'). Говорит is 3rd person singular present of говорить ('he says / he speaks'), used here idiomatically to indicate that he begins to speak at length; the following repetitions intensify that sense.
segments.p1-10-g114: Repeated говорит is the same present-tense verb used iteratively to imitate continuous talking; each repetition increases the sense of ongoing or excessive speech.
segments.p1-10-g115: Final говорит — third repetition — completes the stylistic piling-up, emphasizing 'he talks and talks and talks.'
segments.p1-10-g46: Не все ли равно is a fixed rhetorical question meaning "isn't it all the same?"; ли is an enclitic particle that turns the phrase into a question and равно functions adverbially here ("the same/it makes no difference").
segments.p1-10-g47: если begins a conditional clause "if/whether"; он is the third-person pronoun (referring back to whatever 'he/it' means in context); существует is present tense, 3rd singular, indicative "exists"; в моих желаниях is a prepositional phrase: в + dative? (here prepositional plural) meaning "in my desires" — mental/inner existence.
segments.p1-10-g48: или is the coordinating conjunction "or," used to present an alternative or reformulation of the preceding idea.
segments.p1-10-g49: лучше сказать is a parenthetical correction: лучше (comparative/adverb) + infinitive сказать — literally "better to say" (i.e. "more precisely").
segments.p1-10-g50: существует repeats the verb in present 3rd singular; the subject is the same он (elliptical), so this is an abbreviated restatement "(he) exists."
segments.p1-10-g51: пока introduces a temporal clause meaning "while / as long as"; существуют is present 3rd plural "exist" referring to мои желания (my desires), with мои desires as the grammatical subject in nominative plural.
segments.p1-10-g52: Может быть is a modal phrase meaning "maybe/perhaps"; может is from мочь used here for possibility and быть is the infinitive 'to be', forming the idiom.
segments.p1-10-g53: вы is the polite/plural 'you'; опять is the adverb "again"; смеетесь is present 2nd-person plural of смеяться, here probably meaning "are laughing" or "are mocking" (context decides tone).
segments.p1-10-g54: Извольте is a polite/ironic imperative from изволить (literally 'deign'), used here sarcastically as an invitation: "do be so kind — laugh"; смеяться is the infinitive used as the verbal complement of the imperative.
segments.p1-10-g55: я (I) is subject; все насмешки (all mockeries) is the direct object in accusative plural; приму is the perfective future 1st singular "I will accept/take"; и все-таки не скажу links with conjunction и and the adverbial phrase все-таки ("still/nevertheless") followed by the future negative не скажу "I will not say" — setting up the subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-10-g56: что is the complementizer "that" introducing reported content; я is subject; сыт is a short-form adjective/predicate meaning "sated/filled" (used predicatively: "that I am satisfied/satiated").
segments.p1-10-g57: когда introduces a temporal clause "when/while"; the word order я есть хочу (instead of modern я хочу есть) is a marked/inverted order: есть is the infinitive 'to eat' and хочу is 'I want', so the clause means "when I want to eat."
segments.p1-10-g58: все-таки (here split into two tokens) functions as a discourse adverb meaning "nevertheless/still"; знаю is present 1st singular "I know" — introducing the speaker's conviction.
segments.p1-10-g59: что introduces the content clause "that"; я is subject; не успокоюсь is future reflexive of успокоить/успокоиться, meaning "I will not rest/become satisfied"; на компромиссе uses на + prepositional to indicate "with/by a compromise" (i.e. 'relying on a compromise').
segments.p1-10-g60: на беспрерывном периодическом нуле is a nominal phrase headed by на + prepositional ("on/at a ..."); беспрерывном (uninterrupted) and периодическом (periodic) are adjectives in prepositional singular agreeing with нуле (zero); the oxymoronic phrase "uninterrupted periodic zero" is the object of на.
segments.p1-10-g61: потому только is a causal phrase meaning "for that very reason/only because"; потому is "because/for that reason" and только "only/merely" — together they introduce the motive clause that follows.
segments.p1-10-g62: что introduces the explanatory clause "that"; он is the impersonal/neutral pronoun referring back to the compromise/zero; существует is present "exists"; по законам природы uses по + dative plural (законам) to mean "according to the laws of nature" (природы in genitive). The final существует действительно repeats the assertion with действительно "indeed/actually" for emphasis.
segments.p1-10-g63: Я is subject; не приму is future 1st singular of принять ("I will not accept"); за introduces the role/characterization here ("as"/"for"), so за венец желаний моих = "as the crown/pinnacle of my desires" (венец + genitive желаний моих). The dash introduces the appositive капитальный дом (a substantial/structural house).
segments.p1-10-g64: This is a string of modifiers introduced by с (with): с квартирами (instrumental plural, "with apartments") для бедных жильцов ("for poor tenants"); по контракту на тысячу лет = "by/under a contract for a thousand years" (по + dative meaning 'under/according to' used with контракту); и на всякий случай = "and, just in case" (на + accusative/phrase meaning 'for the sake of/in case'); с зубным врачом Вагенгеймом на вывеске = "with the dentist Wagengheim on the sign" (с + instrumental for accompaniment, на + prepositional for location on the sign).
segments.p1-10-g93: A single-word discourse marker meaning 'on the contrary' used to introduce a contrast with the previous statement.
segments.p1-10-g94: 'я бы' = conditional ('I would'); 'дал себе отрезать' is a causative/permissive construction literally 'would give myself to have (something) cut off' meaning 'would have (someone) cut off my...'; 'совсем' modifies the action ('completely'); 'язык' is the direct object 'tongue'.
segments.p1-10-g95: Prepositional phrase 'из одной благодарности' = 'out of (mere) gratitude'; 'из' = 'out of/from', and 'благодарности' is a noun in the genitive expressing motive.
segments.p1-10-g96: 'если б только' = 'if only' (conditional wish); 'устроилось' is reflexive perfective in past form used impersonally here = 'could be/is arranged' or 'turned out so'; 'так' = 'so/that way'.
segments.p1-10-g97: 'чтоб' introduces the desired/resulting clause 'so that'; 'мне самому' is dative experiencer with intensive 'myself'; 'уже более никогда' is a cluster functioning as 'never again' (уже + более both reinforce cessation); 'не хотелось' is the impersonal past of 'хотеться' with negation = 'would not want/feel like'; 'его' = 'it' (the tongue); 'высовывать' imperfective infinitive 'to stick out' (habitual/repeated action).
segments.p1-10-g98: Rhetorical question 'Какое мне дело до того' = 'What concern/business is it of mine regarding that?'; 'мне' is dative (to me), 'дело' = 'matter/concern', 'до того' = 'as to that'.
segments.p1-10-g99: Two coordinated 'что' clauses giving the content: first 'что так невозможно устроить' = 'that it is impossible to arrange it that way' ('так' = 'that way', 'невозможно' = predicate 'impossible', 'устроить' = infinitive 'to arrange'); second 'и что надо довольствоваться квартирами' = 'and that one must be content with apartments' ('надо' = impersonal modal 'one must', 'довольствоваться' = 'be content with').
segments.p1-10-g100: 'Зачем' = 'what for/why' (rhetorical); 'же' is an emphatic particle; 'я' subject; 'устроен' is a short passive participle meaning 'am arranged/constituted/endowed' (state/result); 'с такими желаниями' = 'with such desires' (preposition 'с' + instrumental).
segments.p1-10-g101: 'Неужели' is a rhetorical particle meaning 'really?/could it be?'; 'ж' adds emphasis; 'я' subject; 'для того только и устроен' = 'made/arranged only for that purpose' (для того = 'for that', 'только и' emphasizes exclusivity), 'устроен' passive/short participle 'made/constituted'.
segments.p1-10-g102: 'чтоб' introduces the purpose/result clause 'in order to / so as to'; 'дойти' (perfective) = 'reach/come to'; 'до' governs the noun 'заключения' (genitive) = 'to the conclusion'.
segments.p1-10-g103: 'что' introduces the content clause: 'that all my устройство (my whole arrangement/constitution) is one "надувание"'; 'все мое устройство' = 'my entire make-up/organization', 'одно' here functions as 'merely/only', and 'надувание' literally 'inflation/puffing up' used metaphorically for pretence or puffery.
segments.p1-10-g78: А here is a connective particle meaning 'but/and' that opens the sentence; покамест is an adverb 'for the time being'; the clause has the subject pronoun я + adverb ещё 'still' and the present-tense verb живу 'I live', followed by the coordinating conjunction и and another present-tense verb желаю 'I desire/wish' — so the whole segment reads 'But for the time being I still live and wish.'
segments.p1-10-g79: Да here functions as an optative particle meaning 'may/let' to introduce a curse/wish; отсохни is the imperative (perfective) 'wither/dry up' addressed to the hand; у меня рука literally 'at me a hand' = 'my hand' (possessive expressed with у + genitive). The phrase is an oath: 'may my hand wither.'
segments.p1-10-g80: Коль is a colloquial/archaic conditional 'if'; the subject я + the particle хоть (here 'even/at least') combine with один кирпичик 'one little brick' as the direct object; на такой капитальный дом uses на with a purpose/benefactive sense 'for/toward such a substantial house'; принесу is 1st person singular future perfective 'I will bring'. Grammatically it is a conditional clause tied to the preceding optative wish ('may my hand wither if I bring...').
segments.p1-10-g81: Не смотрите на то is an idiomatic imperative phrase meaning 'do not pay attention to that / do not mind the fact'; смотрите is the 2nd-person plural imperative of смотреть and на то is the accusative demonstrative phrase 'to that/fact.'
segments.p1-10-g82: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that…'; я is the subject; давеча is a temporal adverb 'recently/the other day'; сам is an emphatic particle 'myself' (stressing that it was he personally); хрустальное здание is the accusative object 'the crystal building'; отверг is past masculine singular 'rejected.' Word order places the verb after the object, as common in Russian narrative.
segments.p1-10-g83: Единственно 'only/merely' modifies the reason phrase; по той причине is a fixed prepositional phrase 'for that reason/on that account' (по + dative), introducing the explanation that follows.
segments.p1-10-g84: Что here begins the explanatory clause. Его is the accusative pronoun 'it' (object of подразнить); нельзя будет is an impersonal future construction meaning 'it will be impossible / one will not be able to'; языком is the instrumental 'with the tongue' (literally 'by tongue'); подразнить is the infinitive 'to provoke/tease.' Together: 'that it would be impossible to provoke with the tongue.'
segments.p1-10-g85: Я это говорил is a simple past statement (говорил = 'I said' / 'I was saying'). Это is a demonstrative pronoun referring back to the preceding remark; placement Я это говорил is neutral/stressed word order (subject – object – verb). Вовсе не потому is an emphatic negated phrase: вовсе strengthens the negation не, and потому introduces a reason clause (completed by the following что).
segments.p1-10-g86: Что is the conjunction 'that' introducing the reason clause. Уж так is an adverbial intensifier (уж = indeed/stronger emphasis; так = so/that way) modifying люблю. Люблю is present tense 'I like/am fond of' (not the stronger 'love' here), and мой язык выставлять is an infinitival construction with мой язык as the (partitive/genitive after negation) object of выставлять ('to display/expose'). The phrase means 'that I so like to display my tongue,' i.e. 'it wasn't because I enjoyed showing my tongue.'
segments.p1-10-g87: Я is the subject pronoun 'I' standing alone with a following pause (comma) for emphasis; it simply marks the speaker as the doer of the forthcoming action.
segments.p1-10-g88: Можно read as the parenthetical modal phrase может быть ('perhaps, maybe'). Here it's split: может (modal verb/possibility) + быть (infinitive 'to be'); together they form the adverb 'perhaps' and function as a hedge about the following statement.
segments.p1-10-g89: На то только и сердился is an idiomatic structure: на то = 'about that'/'for that', только и ... = 'only/merely ...' and сердился is past tense 'was angry'. The construction means 'I was angry only about that' (i.e. that alone made me angry).
segments.p1-10-g90: Что begins a subordinate relative/explanatory clause ('that the fact that...'). Такого modifies здания: такого здания = 'such a building' or 'a building of that kind' (такого stresses the type/quality). Здания is genitive singular (object of the later из всех ваших зданий).
segments.p1-10-g91: Которому (dative relative pronoun 'to/for which') + бы (conditional/modal particle) + можно было (impersonal 'it would have been possible') form a conditional possibility ('to which it might have been possible'). И не выставлять языка combines и (emphatic 'even/also') with the infinitive не выставлять ('not to display') and языка in the genitive — here the genitive marks the direct object in a negated/partitive sense (common after negation). The whole phrase: 'to which it would have been possible not even to display the tongue.'
segments.p1-10-g92: Из всех ваших зданий до сих пор не находится is an impersonal clause meaning 'has not been found among all your buildings up to now.' Из всех ваших зданий = 'out of/all of your buildings'; до сих пор = 'up until now' (сиx is an archaic/demonstrative form in this fixed phrase); не находится = 'is not found/is not located' (here 'is not to be found').

sentences.p1-10-s5: "Yes," I answer, "if one had to live only in order not to get wet."
sentences.p1-10-s6: But what can be done if I got it into my head that people don't live for that alone, and that if one is to live at all, one ought to live in mansions.
sentences.p1-10-s7: This is my will, this is my desire.
sentences.p1-10-s8: You will scrape it out of me only when you change my desires.
sentences.p1-10-s17: Destroy my desires, erase my ideals, show me something better, and I will follow you.
sentences.p1-10-s18: You will probably say that it's not worth getting involved; but in that case, after all, I could answer you the same.
sentences.p1-10-s19: We reason seriously; and if you do not wish to honor me with your attention, then I will not bow.
sentences.p1-10-s1: You believe in a crystal building, forever indestructible — that is, in such a one to which it will be impossible to furtively stick out your tongue or even show a fig‑sign in your pocket.
sentences.p1-10-s2: Well, as for me, perhaps that is precisely why I fear this building — that it is crystal and forever indestructible, and that it will be impossible even to furtively stick out one’s tongue at it.
sentences.p1-10-s3: You see: if, instead of a palace, there is a henhouse and it begins to rain, I might perhaps even climb into the henhouse to avoid getting soaked — but all the same I shall not take the henhouse for a palace out of gratitude that it saved me from the rain.
sentences.p1-10-s4: You laugh; you even say that in this case a henhouse and a mansion are all the same.
sentences.p1-10-s9: Well then — change them; entice me with something else; give me another ideal.
sentences.p1-10-s10: But for the time being I will not take a chicken coop for a palace.
sentences.p1-10-s11: Let it even be so — that the crystal building is a pouf, that by the laws of nature it isn't borne out, and that I invented it only as a result of my own stupidity, as a consequence of certain old, irrational habits of our generation.
sentences.p1-10-s12: But what business is it of mine that it is not warranted?
sentences.p1-10-s29: Could that really be the whole point?
sentences.p1-10-s31: Well, actually, you know what: I'm convinced that our underground brother must be kept on a tight rein.
sentences.p1-10-s32: He may be able to sit silently in the underground for forty years, but if he ever comes out into the light and breaks through, then he goes on and on talking.
sentences.p1-10-s13: Isn't it all the same whether he exists in my desires, or, better said, exists as long as my desires exist?
sentences.p1-10-s15: Go ahead and laugh; I'll accept all mockery and yet I won't say that I'm satisfied when I want to eat; nevertheless I know that I will not be appeased by a compromise, by an uninterrupted periodic zero, simply because it exists according to the laws of nature and actually exists.
sentences.p1-10-s16: I will not accept as the crown of my desires a substantial house — with apartments for poor tenants under a thousand‑year contract and, just in case, with the dentist Wagengheim on the sign.
sentences.p1-10-s25: On the contrary, I would have my tongue completely cut off out of sheer gratitude, if only it could be arranged so that I myself would never again want to stick it out.
sentences.p1-10-s26: What business is it of mine that it can't be arranged that way, and that one must be content with apartments?
sentences.p1-10-s27: Why, then, am I arranged with such desires?
sentences.p1-10-s28: Am I really made only for the purpose of arriving at the conclusion that my whole constitution is nothing but a puffing-up?
sentences.p1-10-s21: But for the time being I still live and wish — may my hand wither if I so much as bring one little brick toward such a substantial house!
sentences.p1-10-s22: Don't mind the fact that I, the other day, myself rejected the crystal building — solely for the reason that it would not be possible to provoke it with the tongue.
sentences.p1-10-s23: I said this not at all because I so love to display my tongue.
sentences.p1-10-s24: Perhaps I was angry only about the fact that, of all your buildings, there has not yet been found such a building to which it might have been possible not even to have to display one's tongue.
